introSections.title: Triangulate one station across three views
introSections.summary: Keep the same station selected while you compare how it presents on CT, in the airway, and on the ultrasound screen.
introSections.takeaway: The goal is transfer: one mental target, three representations.

introSections.title: Use the selector as your anchor
introSections.summary: Switch stations quickly, then read the same laterality, zone, and memory cue across the tri-view cards and checklist.
introSections.takeaway: If orientation slips, reset with the selected station chip and its confusion pair.

introSections.title: Test recognition by representation
introSections.summary: The challenge rotates CT, bronchoscopy, and ultrasound prompts so recall is not tied to just one view.
introSections.takeaway: Immediate feedback plus local accuracy tracking shows which stations still blur together.

reviewPrompts: Can you move from CT to airway to ultrasound without changing the target station?
Which confusion pair still slows you down when the representation changes?
Can you name one airway landmark and one ultrasound cue for each hilar station?
extensionNote: Future approved CT, bronchoscopy, and ultrasound assets can replace the placeholders by swapping the asset keys referenced from the shared station schema while keeping the explorer copy, checklist content, and challenge items intact.
